title: PPT Generation Brief
purpose: Reusable instruction file. Upload this alongside a context block and Claude will produce a consulting-grade deck outline + per-slide content. The JSON is self-documenting — every key explains what Claude should do with it.

how_to_use.step_1: Fill in the `user_context` block below (or paste your own context as a separate message when you upload this file).
how_to_use.step_2: Upload this file and say: 'Use this brief to build a deck for <goal>.'
how_to_use.step_3: Claude returns a deck outline first (titles + one-line message per slide), you confirm or edit, then Claude expands each slide into full content matching `slide_structure.per_slide_schema`.
how_to_use.step_4: Optional: paste the expanded content into the compplan Proposals page and use 'Refine until perfect' to polish visuals.

user_context._comment: Fill in as many of these as you can. Leave blanks where irrelevant. Claude will ask follow-ups only for gaps it actually needs.
user_context.deck_goal: e.g. 'Convince a €40M PE portco CEO to sign a 12-week commercial diagnostic'

client.recent_events: e.g. just raised €50M, new CEO in Q3

deck_methodology.structure_principle: Pyramid Principle — start with the answer, then support it. Every slide answers ONE question the reader has at that moment. The deck as a whole follows SCQA: Situation → Complication → Question → Answer → Proof.
deck_methodology.action_titles: Every slide title must be an ACTION TITLE — a full sentence that states the insight, not just the topic. BAD: 'Market Overview'. GOOD: 'The commercial engine is leaking ~€6M/year, concentrated in 3 regions'.
deck_methodology.one_message_per_slide: If you can't say the point of a slide in one sentence, the slide is doing two jobs — split it.

deck_outline_template._comment: Claude should adapt this skeleton to the user's context. Slides can be dropped / merged / added, but the overall narrative arc (setup → problem → solution → proof → ask) must survive.

slides.purpose: Set the stage: who this is for, what it's about, when.
Reader's navigation map — one line per section.
Answer first. The ONE slide a CEO would keep if they lost the rest.
Shared baseline: where the client is today, no judgement.
What's changed or what's broken — the trigger for this conversation.
The crisp question the deck answers (often implicit in a diagnostic).
Our recommendation in one sentence + the 3 pillars that support it.
How we'd deliver — phases, weeks, outputs. One row per phase.
Gantt-style timeline with milestones and decision points.
Who does the work. Photos + 1-line credential per person.
2–3 analogous cases with quantified outcomes. No logo soup.
Fee + what's included. Must match verbal conversation.
Concrete asks with owners + dates. Never 'we look forward to hearing from you'.
Anything that would dilute the main flow but a reader might ask for.

per_slide_schema.key_message: One sentence. The takeaway if they read nothing else.
per_slide_schema.layout: full_text | two_column | three_column | chart_left_text_right | timeline | matrix_2x2 | team_grid | kpi_row

sections.heading: short label (2-4 words)
sections.chart_hint: optional — what the chart should show + axes + highlight

per_slide_schema.insight_bar: optional — one sentence at the bottom that says 'so what'
sources: optional data sources, shown in 10px footer
per_slide_schema.builder_notes: any rendering hints (e.g. 'bold the € figure', 'red = over budget')

scoring_dimensions.clarity: 0-25 — can a reader grasp the point in 5 seconds?
scoring_dimensions.relevance: 0-25 — is it specific to THIS client, or generic filler?
scoring_dimensions.persuasion: 0-25 — does it drive the decision forward? Is there a clear 'so what'?

quality_bar.iteration_protocol: Score → read back the lowest dimension → propose a specific fix (not 'improve clarity') → regenerate → re-score. Stop when total ≥ 85 or after 4 rounds.
common_failure_modes: Topic titles instead of action titles ('Approach' vs 'Our 3-phase approach delivers the answer in 10 weeks')
Two messages fighting on one slide — split it
Generic methodology slides that would work for any client
Credentials with logos but no numbers
Pricing slide that doesn't match what you said in the room
Next-steps slide with no owners or dates

phase_1_outline.description: Claude returns this first — a skeleton the user can sanity-check before expensive content generation.

phase_2_full_deck.description: After outline approval, Claude returns one object per slide matching slide_structure.per_slide_schema.

phase_3_html_previews.description: Optional — Claude converts each slide object into self-contained HTML divs (960x540, inline CSS, brand colors, Arial) ready to paste into the compplan Proposals page.

example_call.user_message: Use this brief to build a 10-slide deck. Context: we're pitching Acme GmbH (€120M industrial distributor, PE-owned, new CEO, EBITDA margin dropped from 12 to 8). Goal: sell a 12-week commercial diagnostic, €180k. Audience: CEO (ex-BCG) + CFO. Known objection: they just paid another firm for a strategy review last year.
expected_claude_flow: 1. Ask up to 3 clarifying questions if user_context has critical blanks.
5. On request, generate phase_3_html_previews and run the quality_bar.iteration_protocol until total ≥ 85.
